response: The movie "12 Angry Men" (1957) directed by Sidney Lumet, was largely filmed on a single set representing a jury deliberation room in New York City. The courtroom scenes were shot at the now-defunct E.F. Albee I Theatre (later known as the City Center). However, it's worth noting that almost all of the movie takes place inside this one confined location with only brief exterior shots around the courthouse building. The other film mentioned, "Cronos" (1993) directed by Guillermo del Toro, was shot in Mexico.

response: The filming of "Fight Club" took place mostly in Los Angeles, California, with some scenes shot around downtown LA. The exterior of the house used as Tyler Durden's home is located at 2101 E 7th Street in the Arts District neighborhood of downtown LA. Other locations include the exteriors of a bar on South Figueroa Street and various other spots within the city. The production design by Alex McDowell and set decoration by Victor Zolfo created a distinctive look for the film, which included many grungy and industrial-looking sets to emphasize the themes of consumerism and anti-materialism explored in the movie.
